claim: Elevated temperature accelerates SEI layer growth consuming cyclable lithium
risk_if_wrong: Overoptimistic lifetime predictions and undersized thermal management
recommended_next_step: Use as foundational claim with quantified SEI growth rate model

claim: Electrolyte decomposition and oxidation are primary high-temperature pathways
risk_if_wrong: Underestimates resistance growth from oxidation products

claim: Cathode dissolution releases transition-metal ions incorporated into SEI
risk_if_wrong: Misses non-linear fade trigger at elevated temperature
recommended_next_step: Use with qualification; flagged for future investigation

claim: Loss of active material is a distinct independent primary mechanism
risk_if_wrong: Double-counting capacity loss in degradation models
recommended_next_step: Refine to note synergy with SEI growth; seek source quantifying LAM independently

claim: Lithium plating is a significant high-temperature degradation mechanism
risk_if_wrong: Physically incorrect pathway invalidating model predictions
recommended_next_step: Reject as stated; lithium plating is primarily a low-temperature mechanism
